title: Year-aware FIA rule-exception features and specialists
summary: The current best features approximate dry-race tyre obligations, but FIA rules are conditional: the two-dry-compound obligation is waived once intermediates/wets are used, Monaco has a special 2025 three-set rule, and pre-season testing is a different regime.
rationale: The current best features approximate dry-race tyre obligations, but FIA rules are conditional: the two-dry-compound obligation is waived once intermediates/wets are used, Monaco has a special 2025 three-set rule, and pre-season testing is a different regime. A rule-corrected obligation state should separate strategic stops from purely degradational stops.
implementation_hint: Create causal features for `dry_rule_active_so_far`, `mandatory_compound_used_so_far`, `distinct_slicks_used_so_far`, `owes_dry_change`, `wet_used_so_far`, `monaco_2025_special`, and `is_testing_session`. Train either one global model with these flags or a small specialist blend for dry-race vs wet/testing vs Monaco-2025 rows. Validation trap: build all obligation counters only from prior/current laps within Driver x Race.
expected_effect: Should improve late-race and edge-case ranking where the current slick-count heuristic is too blunt, with upside if public test contains wet/testing/Monaco mixtures.
risk: If Monaco or wet/testing rows are sparse, a specialist can overfit; keep the split coarse and back off to flags if sample counts are low.
